content.content:
1→{
     2→  "name": "@mms/harness-orchestrator",
     3→  "version": "0.1.0",
     4→  "description": "AI Project Manager that orchestrates multiple Claude agents to complete harness tasks in parallel",
     5→  "type": "module",
     6→  "main": "./dist/index.js",
     7→  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
     8→  "bin": {
     9→    "harness": "./dist/cli/index.js",
    10→    "h": "./dist/cli/index.js"
    11→  },
    12→  "scripts": {
    13→    "dev": "tsc --watch",
    14→    "build": "tsc",
    15→    "start": "node dist/cli/index.js",
    16→    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
    17→  },
    18→  "dependencies": {
    19→    "chalk": "^5.4.1",
    20→    "commander": "^13.1.0",
    21→    "ora": "^8.1.1",
    22→    "proper-lockfile": "^4.1.2"
    23→  },
    24→  "devDependencies": {
    25→    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",
    26→    "@types/proper-lockfile": "^4.1.4",
    27→    "typescript": "^5.6.3"
    28→  },
    29→  "engines": {
    30→    "node": ">=18.0.0"
    31→  },
    32→  "keywords": [
    33→    "claude",
    34→    "orchestrator",
    35→    "ai",
    36→    "automation",
    37→    "harness"
    38→  ]
    39→}
    40→

content.text: Vou compilar o projeto e depois instalá-lo globalmente.

content.content:
bun link v1.3.4 (5eb2145b)
Success! Registered "@mms/harness-orchestrator"

To use @mms/harness-orchestrator in a project, run:
  bun link @mms/harness-orchestrator

Or add it in dependencies in your package.json file:
  "@mms/harness-orchestrator": "link:@mms/harness-orchestrator"

toolUseResult.stdout:
bun link v1.3.4 (5eb2145b)
Success! Registered "@mms/harness-orchestrator"

To use @mms/harness-orchestrator in a project, run:
  bun link @mms/harness-orchestrator

Or add it in dependencies in your package.json file:
  "@mms/harness-orchestrator": "link:@mms/harness-orchestrator"

content.text: O package foi registrado com `bun link`. Agora preciso verificar se os comandos `harness` e `h` estão disponíveis globalmente.

input.command: which harness || echo "harness command not in PATH"
input.description: Check if harness command is available

content.content:
Usage: harness|h [options] [command]

AI Project Manager for harness task orchestration

Options:
  -V, --version                          output the version number
  -h, --help                             display help for command

Commands:
  analyze [features-path]                Analyze dependency graph and show execution plan
  status [features-path]                 Show current progress status
  orchestrate [options] [features-path]  Run full orchestration with parallel agents
  dashboard [options] [features-path]    Show real-time dashboard (attaches to running orchestration)
  verify [options] [features-path]       Verify feature completion and show verification status
  rollback [options] [features-path]     Rollback a feature to its previous state
  branches [options] [features-path]     List and manage feature branches
  init [options] [project-path]          Initialize a new harness project with standard structure
  continue [options] [project-path]      Continue working on the harness (Two-Agent Architecture per Anthropic)
  session [options] [project-path]       Create a new session entry in claude-progress.txt
  log [options] <message>                Append a quick note to claude-progress.txt
  progress [options] [project-path]      Display parsed progress file with statistics
  test [options] [project-path]          Generate visual testing commands for Playwright MCP
  commit [options] [project-path]        Validate and prepare commit following harness protocol
  pre-commit [options] [project-path]    Run pre-commit validation (for git hooks)
  doctor [options] [project-path]        Validate harness project structure and health
  help [command]                         display help for command

content.text:
Pronto! O package `@mms/harness-orchestrator` foi instalado globalmente com sucesso.

**Comandos disponíveis:**
- `harness` - comando completo
- `h` - alias curto

**Localização:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/.bun/bin/harness`

Você pode usar qualquer um dos comandos de qualquer diretório:
```bash
harness analyze      # Analisar grafo de dependências
harness status       # Ver progresso atual
harness orchestrate  # Executar orquestração
harness --help       # Ver todos os comandos
```
